slides.header: Lima Dam.  Reservoir went on for 20 miles
After a mostly flat morning could see the Tetons on the horizon
Up ahead - Red Rock pass and leaving Montana.
The climb up to Red Rock pass was not so bad. I was getting use to the hills.
One of my sleeping options.  Things got better
Taking a Scenic Byway instead of the sand
slides.caption: Tetons from 40 miles

slides.header: Took a side trip to visit this spectacular falls
slides.caption: Upper Mesa Falls
Lower Mesa Falls
The flat gradual part of Ashton Flagg Ranch Road

slides.header: Ashton Flagg Ranch Road
slides.caption: It is 36 miles of gravel road - sometimes washboarded, sometimes rutted, often windy, always narrow.

slides.header: Grand Teton NP
The Oxbow in 1993
slides.caption: From an earlier trip - still beautiful

slides.header: Togwotee Pass
slides.caption: 20 miles and 2,800 feet up from Moran, WY.

slides.header: Wind River Lake
slides.caption: Surrounded by some amazing rock formations.

slides.header: Wind River Lake
slides.caption: Nice spot to have a break after a long climb

slides.header: Heading to Union Pass
slides.caption: Starting the day the same way I started most days - me and my shadow.

slides.header: Some nice 8-10% grades along the way
The views opened up all the back to the Tetons
slides.caption: View west

slides.header: At the top of Devils Hole Trail
slides.caption: View east

slides.header: After the two climbs I rode along the divide for miles
slides.caption: Union Pass area - view west

slides.header: near Union Pass
slides.caption: Union Pass area - view north

slides.header: ... for miles and miles
slides.caption: I was amazed again and again during the five plus hours it took me to go through this section.

slides.header: Rolling toward Pinedale - back in the desert
slides.caption: The last 40 miles was rolling downhill and south through wide open spaces. The Wind River Range was to the east

slides.header: The first 35 miles of the day were hot but reasonably flat.
Once agin riding along the divide for miles
The Big Sandy River
Along the road to South Pass City, a restored mining town.
Atlantic City - stopped for the day
slides.caption: Miners Grub Stake - the tepee was taken so I ended up at a home nearby.

slides.header: 100 miles thru the desert
Andrew on the way to the Stillwater River
slides.caption: It was the last reliable water

slides.header: By 9:30 I was alone for the day
Flat - but the roads not
slides.caption: The double track was rutted and difficult to get a good line through.

slides.header: There were no cattle guards, fences or signs.
slides.caption: Wild horses along the way
He really looked angry.

slides.header: about 10 miles that felt more remote than almost any other spot on the ride.
slides.caption: Picture from Jill Horner"s story also gives a sense of what I was riding through.

slides.header: Wamsutter, WY
slides.caption: Back to civilization or at least a Truck stop along Interstate 80

slides.header: More desert south of Wamsutter, WY
slides.caption: Going south there were many trucks building, widening or maintaining the gravel road.

slides.header: Afternoon in the desert
slides.caption: Heading toward these mountains in Colorado
another fence

slides.header: Savery, WY - 5 miles to the Colorado border
slides.caption: The museum basement had various items in the fridge.  I had a couple yogurts and at least 4 cheese sticks to go with 2 cans of Coke.Their break room was air conditioned so I stayed for a while.

slides.header: Lots of ups and down on the way to Brush Mountain Lodge.
slides.caption: Entering Colorado - destination is 12 miles up Slater Creek

slides.header: Along Slater Creek
slides.caption: It was hard to get a sense of how bad this was going to be.
The ride up to Brush Mountain Lodge was slow and painful but it was a ride and not a walk

slides.header: Brush Mountain Lodge
slides.caption: Before I could sit down I had been offered Lemonade and Cheeseburgers for my belly and ice for my knees.

slides.header: Happy Birthday Jim
Leaving Brush Moutain and continuing up Slater Creek
It was a beautiful morning and only 50+ miles to Steamboat Springs
Aspen Alley
Still climbing
Late in the morning
Lunch stop
slides.caption: Downhill from here to Steamboat
The first 20 miles out of Steamboat was developed with homes and ranches all around.
Road leading to Yampo River Dam. I was supposed to ride across the top of it.

slides.header: toward Lynx Pass
slides.caption: This was a long climb which put my goal of reaching Dillon in doubt

slides.header: Finally on top of Lynx Pass
slides.caption: Top surprised me - I thought I had a few more miles to go

slides.header: Gore Pass
slides.caption: After struggling on Lynx I chose Gore Pass instead of through Radium, an old uranium mine.

slides.header: After Gore Pass the 14,000 ft mountains came into view
slides.caption: It was about three miles to the pass and then a wonderful 16 mile coast down to US Hwy 40.

slides.header: 36 miles to Silverthorn - starting at 3pm
slides.caption: This was torture - gradually up along the Green Mountain Reservoir

slides.header: End of a 100 mile day
slides.caption: With less than 15 miles to go I was able to convince myself I could probably walk the last few miles.

slides.header: Lake Dillon
slides.caption: Starting the day at the Dillon Lake by my hotel

slides.header: Looking down on Breckenridge
slides.caption: The lakes were full

slides.header: Boreas Pass Road
slides.caption: 9 mile climb.    Looking like rain.

slides.header: Boreas Pass - 11,482ft
slides.caption: Highest pass I traveled through

slides.header: Boreas Pass - 11,482ft
slides.caption: Railroad car to show that there used to be a railroad thru here.

slides.header: The south side of Boreas Pass
slides.caption: From here on it would rain almost every afternoon

slides.header: from Como to Hartsel a 10 mile stretch of washboard
slides.caption: There were rain clouds all around but the rain held off until I reach the Highline Cafe & Saloon in Hartsel.
Rainbow over bustling downtown of Hartsel, CO.

slides.header: Sunset on my home for the previous night
slides.caption: The town gazebo - safe and sound

slides.header: Sunrise in Hartsel
Just about a traffic jam of bikes
slides.caption: People coming and going all different ways from Florida to Alaska
I think Ive been down this road before.

slides.header: Trucks where they shouldn"t be - Boots, jacket but no driver
slides.caption: Somehow this truck was gone a few hours later.  The mud was not

slides.header: From outside Salina
slides.caption: Dropping almost 3,000 ft over 12 miles.

slides.header: Repairs were needed before our big climb
slides.caption: Poncha Springs Post Office was our repair stop
Stopping for repairs after just getting started

slides.header: Once off the main road it looked like it would be a beautiful day
27 miles - 4,000 ft
slides.caption: We got this

slides.header: On top of Marshall Pass
On top of Marshall Pass  - Rob, Andrew and Jim
Home for an afternoon nap
slides.caption: Colorado Trail - Is this a shortcut?

slides.header: Home for the night - A leaky tepee
slides.caption: Room for three people and bikes.

slides.header: Leaving the Teepee on an overcast day
slides.caption: The initial 13 miles on US50 to Doyleville where I turned south onto gravel roads.

slides.header: 20 miles of gravel
slides.caption: Some long slow climbs in this section of gravel prior to getting back onto CO 114.

slides.header: From Doyleville (8,100 ft) to North Cochetopa (10,135)
slides.caption: I reached the pass about noon and rejoined the TD route at the low point before heading up to Carnero Pass.

slides.header: Lighting and Thunder on way to Carero Pass
slides.caption: The pass was 12 miles out and there was rain all around.

slides.header: A little rain 3 miles from the top
Second 10,000 foot pass of the day.
slides.caption: Trail magic just before the top gave me some water and returned my headlamp

slides.header: Some great canyons and hodos on the way down.
slides.caption: Each time I stopped the rain would come in behind me and I would ride as fast as I could to get out in front of it.

slides.header: Literally blown into town
slides.caption: As I approached Del Norte the sun broke through the clouds in a couple spots.

slides.header: Woke up to rain
slides.caption: I went to the Amish bakery and bought a cinnamon bun and headed out to do my laundry.

slides.header: Two nights at the Windsor Hotel
slides.caption: I had a delicious meal (Wild Boar) and fantastic desert to celebrate my day off.
I spent the afternoon plotting my course in and around the remainder of the tour divide.  I was trying to avoid the afternoon rain and wind.
An afternoon snack of Strawberry Rubarb pie from The Columbine.
